text:
**There is a genuine gap to address, and the cleanest fix is the descent route you already sketched in your Round-6 note.** The exact step needing tightening is "(2) follows from (1) by separate weak-* continuity": (1) is a $B(L^2N)$-identity, "$\cL_\cH$" lives in $\cA_N$, and the canonical map $B(L^2N)\to\cA_N$ is u.c.p., not multiplicative. Citing "composition of DKEP rectangular normal bimodules" passes the buck to DKEP ¬ß5 in a way most readers will not unpack.

**Concrete restructuring.** Move the support calculation to $B(L^2N)^{**}$ (multiplication honest there) and cut by $p_{\rm nor}$ once at the end.

The sandwich is equivalent ‚Äî using $q_X=pe_X$ and $[p,e_X]=0$ ‚Äî to the right-support identity in $B(L^2N)^{**}$:
$$e_\cK e_\cH = e_\cK e_\cH\, e_Y\qquad\text{for an appropriate fusion target }Y.$$

**Reduction lemma.** If $\K_X,\K_Y$ are hereditary $N$,$JNJ$-bimodule $C^*$-subalgebras of $B(L^2N)$ and $\K_X\K_Y\K_X\subset\K_Z$ in $B(L^2N)$, then in $B(L^2N)^{**}$
$$e_X(e_YB(L^2N)^{**}e_Y)e_X\subset e_ZB(L^2N)^{**}e_Z.$$
Proof: approximate $a\in e_YB(L^2N)^{**}e_Y$ weak-$*$ by bounded nets from $\K_Y$, sandwich by approximate units of $\K_X$, pass to weak-$*$ limits. Cut by $p_{\rm nor}$ to descend.

**Now the fusion input is purely $B(L^2N)$-level.** Your Round-6 triple-coefficient identity
$$(T_{\xi_1}^*T_{\xi_2})(T_{\eta_1}^*T_{\eta_2})(T_{\xi_3}^*T_{\xi_4})
=T_{\xi_1\otimes\bar\xi_2\otimes\eta_1}^*T_{\xi_4\otimes\bar\xi_3\otimes\eta_2}$$
is a literal product in $B(L^2N)$, giving $\K_{X_\cH}\K_{X_\cK}\K_{X_\cH}\subset\K_{X_{\cH\otimes\bar\cH\otimes\cK}}$ on the nose. With $\cH=\cH_W$ self-conjugate (graph-product case), $\cH\otimes\bar\cH\otimes\cK\cong\cH_W\otimes_M\cH_W\otimes_M\cH_U$, and CDHJKN fusion applied twice decomposes this into $\cH_Z$'s with $Z\subset U\cap W$. So $q_W(q_U\cA_M q_U)q_W\subset q_{U\cap W}\cA_M q_{U\cap W}$.

**Two practical notes.**

(i) *Palindromic induction at the $e_X$ level.* Establish $e_{U_m}\cdots e_{U_1}\cdots e_{U_m}$ has range $\subset\mathrm{range}(e_I)$ in $B(L^2N)^{**}$ by induction, then cut by $p_{\rm nor}$ once at the end. Interleaving the cut step-by-step is exactly where the multiplicativity worry could re-bite.

(ii) *Can the binary-fusion / left-ideal mechanism in current answer.tex be salvaged?* Yes, if you commit to interpreting "$\cL_\cK\cL_\cH$" as a product in $B(L^2N)^{**}$ (not in $\cA_N$) and verify the binary $\K_{X_\cK}\K_{X_\cH}\subset\K_{X_{\cH\otimes\cK}}$ at the $B(L^2N)$ level. Uncertain: I have not independently checked that this binary inclusion follows from $T_{\xi\otimes\eta}=T_\eta T_\xi$ alone ‚Äî the type of $T_\xi$ (in $B(L^2N)$ vs.\ $B(L^2N,\cH)$) matters here, and the triple-product route sidesteps the question entirely.

**Bottom line.** The Lemma is correct; the proof needs about one paragraph reorienting "(2)" as an $e_X$-level computation in $B(L^2N)^{**}$ via the triple-product identity, followed by one $p$-cut. The "weak-$*$ closed normal left ideal in $\cA_N$" language is what's drawing fire; trading it for "open supports of hereditary $C^*$-subalgebras of $B(L^2N)$ projected to $B(L^2N)^{**}$" makes the multiplicativity question moot, and uses only the structural input (`$[p,e_X]=0$`) you already cite.
